entry.title: Week 15, 2026: Active week across layout polymorphism, unboxed types, Flambda2 reaper, backend codegen, and runtime improvements.
entry.content:
Summary

Layout polymorphism, Flambda2 reaper refactoring, unboxed type representation changes, and backend improvements were the main threads of activity.

Activity

  • Draft SSA variant of the CFG backend opened by @ttebbi-janestreet #5810
  • Draft deletion of empty loops using induction variable analysis built on top of the SSA CFG, opened by Mark Shinwell #5840
  • Skip caml_modify for non_pointer values opened by Ryan Tjoa, rebasing Zack Eisbach's earlier work #5831
  • Represent all-value/void mixed records and variants as uniform blocks opened by Ryan Tjoa #5774
  • Represent float64 records as mixed blocks and add [@@represent_as_float_array] attribute opened by Ryan Tjoa #5796
  • Require [@@flatten_floats] attribute on mixed float-float64 records opened by Ryan Tjoa #5795
  • Move Flambda2 export info out of .cmx inline Marshal block into a section, opened by Nicolas Trangez #5809
  • Structured Fexpr primitive representation for improved parameterization of fexprs opened by Keryan Didier #5825
  • Reaper code size computation when rebuilding terms opened by Nathanaëlle Courant #5793
  • Allow immediates in upstream-compatible code merged #5699
  • ikinds on minus24 backend feature merged #5159
  • Reaper: dep_solver.ml split into separate modules merged by Nathanaëlle Courant #5776
  • Reaper cleanup after dep_solver split merged #5787
  • Patricia trees: additional union_total function families to avoid allocations merged #5803
  • Simplify lpoly generalize via path compression merged by Zesen Qian #5792
  • Scannable axes compute nullability and separability merged #5169
  • update_level corrected to ignore level when sort variable contents is Some _ merged #5791
  • Layout var instantiation changed to fatal_error rather than Not_found merged #5817
  • moregeneral always restores current_level merged #5816
  • Layout instantiation restricted to static values merged #5783
  • Attempt to reset tick thread after fork() merged by Aspen Smith #5829
  • Additional compactions in the dissector merged #5790
  • Fine-grained profiling added to the linker scan-file loop merged by Nicolas Trangez #5808
  • Fix annoying REPL issue with metaprogramming syntax directive merged by Andrej Ivašković #5801
  • Graceful reporting of unsupported language features in runtime metaprogramming merged #5823
  • Handle staging in Printtyp more carefully merged by Jakub Bachurski #5785
  • Eliminate useless shifts in string/bytes indexed access merged #5746
  • Share code in emitters for data items merged #5794
  • Patricia tree callback modules for function specialisation merged #5770
  • Remove line numbers from assertion failures in tests merged #5782
  • Delete equal_obj and equal_morph, using compare instead merged #5799
  • Review of Traverse and Traverse_acc modules merged #5789 #5797
  • 5.4 merge: various testsuite promotions merged #5757
  • Fix statmemprof exception tests merged #5804
  • Reaper: remove field_{usages,sources}_top merged #5748
  • Simplify: update recursive flag to fix reaper inlining decision merged #5819
  • Avoid persistent env wrapper partial applications, saving ~100mb allocation when compiling typecore.ml merged #5631
entry.summary: Active week across layout polymorphism, unboxed types, Flambda2 reaper, backend codegen, and runtime improvements.

entry.title: Week 14, 2026: A highly active week with major advances in staging/GADTs, abstract kinds, new modes, Flambda2 optimizations, and backend codegen improvements.
entry.content:
Summary

A very busy week with 47 merged PRs spanning staging, modes, Flambda2, reaper, backend codegen, and the ongoing OxCaml 5.4 merge track.

Activity

  • #5774 proposes representing all-value/void mixed records and variants as uniform blocks, bumping mixed block version to v6 and removing the 254-value limit.
  • #5734 adds initial (untested) aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu support in the dissector; a related issue #5732 was opened to track dissector support for arm64 Linux.
  • #5738 proposes improved codegen for int comparisons using XOR before comparison and a better 3-way comparison lowering.
  • #5746 proposes eliminating useless shifts in string/bytes indexed access.
  • #5740 proposes duplicating comparisons in CFG block terminators for select/cmov.
  • #5742 opens work on unwinding support via sframes.
  • #5751 continues work on the n-way join adding extra params for CSE-like variables at join points, a reloaded version of #5733.
  • #5771 fixes a bug in meet for array element kinds in Flambda2.
  • #5770 proposes using callback modules to emulate function specialisation in the patricia tree, targeting a ~1% compiler speedup.
  • #5772 adds most missing primitives to fexpr to support full compiler/stdlib IR dumps.
  • #5761 proposes flipping the default for SPLIT_AROUND_LOOPS regalloc parameter.
  • #5762 proposes enabling prologue shrink-wrap by default.
  • #5753 uses constant propagation to rewrite CFG operations.
  • #5743 adds a CFG pass to move instructions common to several blocks.
  • #5749 splits the CFG preprocessing pass by moving more definitions.
  • #5747 fixes a bug involving effects, callbacks and locals.
  • #5643 continues work on local constraints cannot time travel as part of the staging+GADTs chain.
  • #5667 (draft) proposes refining recursive unboxed product dummy layouts using sort variables.
  • #5752 works on ikinds on minus34 branch.
  • #5755 adds val poly_ support building on the merged let poly_ work.
  • #5737 adds CI configuration with experimental Flambda2 flags for the testsuite.
  • #5641 was merged: GADTs mostly sound with respect to staging, covering parts 1–3 of the staging+GADTs chain.
  • #5629 was merged: abstract kinds with kind_ constraints.
  • #5713 was merged: corrupted and corruptible modes.
  • #5608 was merged: write and writing modes.
  • #5724 was merged: static modules.
  • #5639 was merged: X86 peephole optimization infrastructure and rules.
  • #5764, #5744, #5759 were merged: reaper cleanups including making -dreaper more intuitive, removing {co,}field_{usages,sources}, and adding profile calls in the solver.
  • #5705 was merged: IRC complexity threshold for register allocation.
  • #5728 was merged: new Lfor compilation strategy.
  • #5723 was merged: simplify Cfg_with_layout.insert_block interface.
  • #5623 was merged: CI test for continuation specialization.
  • #5710 was merged: fixes to the JIT and Opttoploop for mdx compatibility.
  • #5765, #5717, #5711, #5729, #5694, #5534 were merged as part of the ongoing OxCaml 5.4 merge track.
entry.summary: A highly active week with major advances in staging/GADTs, abstract kinds, new modes, Flambda2 optimizations, and backend codegen improvements.

entry.title: Week 13, 2026: Active week with 33 merges spanning metaprogramming soundness, mode system renames, ARM64 improvements, and multiple reaper bug fixes.
entry.content:
Summary

Dozens of PRs opened spanning metaprogramming staging, new modes, codegen improvements, and continued 5.4 upstream merge work.

Activity

  • Newly opened metaprogramming PRs this week extend quote/splice soundness: sound moding of quotes and splices by Jakub Bachurski (#5697), quotes of expansive expressions at mode once (#5698), top-level expressions at mode once (#5715), and environment stage tracking in typing with GADT interaction tests (#5641)
  • New mode system PRs opened: write and writing modes by Nailen Matschke (#5608), corrupted and corruptable modes (#5713), poisoned and poisoning modes (draft, #5666), and unique implies uncontended by @julesjacobs (#5716)
  • New codegen and compiler infrastructure PRs opened: CFG SSA reconstruction by @ttebbi-janestreet (#5722), codegen examples for materialized bits (#5727), new Lfor compilation strategy by Mark Shinwell (#5728), flag -thunkify-compilation-unit-initialization by James Rayman (#5720), and static modules by Jack Rickard (#5724)
  • 5.4 upstream merge work continues with multiple ongoing and newly-opened PRs: towards labeled tuples support (#5692), Stdlib iarray (#5693), towards make install for OxCaml 5.4 (#5717), testsuite conflicts (#5711), and runtime merge (#5598) by David Allsopp and Nick Barnes; completed merges include stdlib conflicts (#5616), ocamltest (#5605), debugger (#5617), and miscellany (#5729)
  • Reaper correctness cluster completed: three fixes merged for the Flambda2 reaper — exception parameter deletion bug (#5563), deadlock between uses and sources computation with local fields (#5648), and suppression of value slot stamp printing without the stamps debug flag (#5700), all by Nathanaëlle Courant; also a reaper testcase for #5633 (#5682) and coaccessor/coconstructor renames (#5681)
  • Flambda2 continuation lifting correctness improved with a fix preventing lifting of continuations that may refer to non-lifted continuations by Guillaume Bury (#5689), and a fix for unbound variables in lifted constants by Vincent Laviron (#5725), superseding the earlier approach in #5719
  • n-way join improvements merged: missing-cmx variable scoping fix by Basile Clément (#5679), with a follow-on PR using untagged const false for nullability detection (#5718) opened; related issue #5721 opened reporting missing CSE with n-way join
  • Sort and jkind type system cleanup: generic sort variable simplification by Zesen Qian (#5704), Jkind.Sort.new_var return type corrected to Jkind.Sort.var (#5658), and parameter vs return mode fix (#5596); ongoing: moregeneral should regeneralize layout vars on error (#5691), Texp_apply_layout for layout-poly instantiation (#5634), let poly_ type checking (#5513)
  • Slambda fracturing initial implementation merged — adds the simplest slambda fracturing pass by Jack Rickard (#5544), laying groundwork for more advanced code splitting
  • iarrayLabels.ml sync — fixes propagated from iarray.ml to the labeled variant by David Allsopp (#5683); ongoing Stdlib iarray 5.4 merge work in (#5693)
  • CI and build fixes: dune pinned to 3.20.2 in CI (#5684), Dune 3.22 compilation compatibility fix (#5686), missing compiler-libs-installation.sexp entries added (#5709), macOS compilation fix opened by Amethyst Shen (#5714)
  • Ongoing abstract kinds work by Chris Casinghino: with kind_ constraints (part 3, #5629) and tests for is_really_poly in typecore (#5730); also ongoing zero-alloc argument support (#5493)
  • Ongoing mode polymorphism work by @ainageorges: functional mode polymorphism (#5483), alloc mode (#5482), copy operation (#5481), normal form morphisms (#5453), and printing of polymorphic mode variables (#5488)
  • JIT and Opttoploop fixes for mdx integration opened by Mark Shinwell (#5710); statmemprof comment improvements by Nick Barnes (#5707); custom or_nulls by @julesjacobs (#5668)
  • unsafe_multidomain and do_not_spawn_domains alerts turned off in the test suite by David Allsopp (#5694), touching 108 test files
  • Allow immediates in upstream-compatible code opened by @dkalinichenko-js (#5699); Typedtree constants for unboxed unit and bool by Luke Maurer (#5690); my_region/my_ghost_region parameter merge by Nathanaëlle Courant (#5702)
entry.summary: Active week with 33 merges spanning metaprogramming soundness, mode system renames, ARM64 improvements, and multiple reaper bug fixes.

entry.title: Week 12, 2026: Heavy week of metaprogramming merges, reaper fixes, backend cleanups, and a wave of new mode/unboxed-repr draft PRs.
entry.content:
Summary

34 new PRs opened covering mode bitfields, unboxed reprs, staging+GADTs, x86 optimizations, and a fresh Multicore domains fix.

Activity

  • New metaprogramming work opened: Jakub Bachurski opened a two-part staging+GADTs series — #5641 tracks the environment stage through type-checking and adds exhaustive GADT annotation tests, and #5586 (updated on the parent) makes spliced types non-instantiable to close a soundness hole. He also opened #5670 to support mode annotations under quotes (previously silently dropped), #5674 to raise an error instead of silently dropping jkind annotations in quotes, and #5643 on local constraints not time-travelling through staging.
  • Mode system expansion PRs: @julesjacobs opened a four-part mode-bitfields series (#5671 abstraction barriers, #5672 constant mode product packing, #5673 modality/crossing packing, #5677 comonadic fragment packing) to eliminate allocation in the constant mode representation. Nailen Matschke opened #5666 adding poisoned and poisoning modes as contention/portability analogues of write/observable, and his earlier #5608 (write/observable mode diamond) continued receiving review discussion.
  • New unboxed-repr and layout work: @julesjacobs opened #5678 (draft) adding constructor-level [@repr null/immediate/pointer/value/unboxed] annotations for allocation-free variant encodings, #5667 (draft) fixing recursive unboxed product dummy layout shapes, #5665 adding local abstract kind substitutions, and #5668 enabling user-defined [@@or_null] type declarations with custom constructor names.
  • x86 backend optimization PRs opened: ttebbi-janestreet opened #5635 replacing spill/reload pairs around calls with push/pop instructions (shorter encoding, reduced frame size, gated by -cfg-push-pop-around-calls), #5639 adding x86 peephole optimizations, and #5651 adding an optimization-fuel infrastructure with a Python bisect driver for isolating miscompilations.
  • Multicore and runtime new PRs: Vesa Karvonen opened #5638 to change Multicore to keep domains alive; Stephen Dolan opened #5644 adding Gc.Memprof.participate_globally and #5640 making Atomic.make_contended work on runtime4.
  • Small int / machtype work: James Rayman opened #5664 (draft) adding small-int machtype support and #5676 adding codegen tests for 8-bit and 16-bit integers, complementing the ongoing #5425 draft stopping sign-extension of unboxed small ints.
  • Register allocator improvements opened: Xavier Clerc opened #5656 (early draft) using leaf-function clobber information to reduce spills, #5659 for architecture-dependent spilling costs, and #5632 to replace CFG dumps with a Printcfg module.
  • 5.4 merge progress: Nick Barnes continued advancing #5598 (merge 5.4 runtime, now compiling and linking successfully); #5487 (parsing conflicts) was merged by @dkalinichenko-js.
  • Ongoing mode-solver rewrite: @ainageorges continued review discussions on the mode-variable-levels chain (#5478, #5479, #5480, #5481) with review from @julesjacobs.
  • Zero-alloc on function parameters: Andrej Ivašković continued iterating on #5493 with review from Chris Casinghino, addressing arity inference, backend test coverage, and signature matching for zero_alloc parameter annotations.
  • Closed without merge: Several old test-baseline PRs from Nick Roberts (#2537, #3163, #3171, #3175, #3176, #3239) and #5569 (remove legacy mode syntax from AST) were closed.
entry.summary: Heavy week of metaprogramming merges, reaper fixes, backend cleanups, and a wave of new mode/unboxed-repr draft PRs.

entry.title: Week 11, 2026: A highly active week spanning type system advances, reaper stabilization, backend optimizations, and continued OCaml 5.4 merge work.
entry.content:
Summary

Twenty-eight PRs were merged this week covering typing improvements, reaper stabilization, 5.4 upstream merge progress, and backend infrastructure work.

Activity

  • #5608 adds write and observable modes to the mode system, introducing a partial order diamond where read and write are incomparable, with a bit-vector encoding in the solver for performance; this is under active review awaiting feedback from layout-poly maintainers.
  • #5627 proposes optimized Patricia trees that bitpack two ints together and specialize common operations, delivering 3–11% compile-time speedups and up to 19% reduced allocations on key compiler files.
  • #5629 opens abstract kinds part 3 with with kind_ constraints, the final planned feature before announcing abstract kinds publicly, including full cycle checking to prevent recursive kinds.
  • #5630 hoists primitive repr tables out of prim_has_valid_reprs, avoiding repeated reconstruction of large String.Map values and achieving up to 1.79x faster compilation of modules with many external declarations.
  • #5623 adds a CI workflow for continuation specialization, expanding test coverage for the match-in-match optimization.
  • #5615 removes the ~level parameter from jkind comparison/violation APIs by introducing a level_fresh sentinel, significantly simplifying call sites across 14 files.
  • #5601 extends with-bounds to support external modalities, allowing types like type 'a t : value mod portable external_ with 'a @@ external_.
  • #5602 adds syntax for mode constraints without a type — (expr : @ modes) — and updates pprintast to use the new syntax, with user-facing documentation updated.
  • #5569 works to remove legacy mode syntax from the AST, making pprintast emit new-style mode annotations and addressing roundtrip issues with local_ desugaring.
  • #5577 passes magic number versions via ./configure, simplifying the process of minting new tags without needing a separate magic number bump.
  • #5628 was merged so ocamldep now scans with kind annotations for dependency tracking.
  • #5625 reverted the unboxed GADT jkind projection fix (#5580) after an internal build failure, and #5580 was subsequently re-merged after verifying the internal codebase.
  • #5624 bumped magic numbers for 5.2.0minus-34.
  • #5517 merged abstract kinds part 2.5 adding kind declarations in recursive modules with cycle detection.
  • #5498 merged the canonical layout poly value description, implementing parsing, printing, and inclusion checks for layout-polymorphic value signatures.
  • #5575 fixed buggy abstract kinds error messages including a fatal_error triggered during error printing.
  • Multiple reaper fixes were merged: #5593 adds a zero_alloc hack for local fields, #5611 improves error messages, #5612 handles unboxed arguments with no sources, #5619 enables local fields under -O4, #5595 wraps Not_found with proper fatal errors, and #5590 stops exporting toplevel functions in cmx.
  • #5588 added a reaper + local value slots CI job to expand coverage.
  • #5609 improved the gather regalloc fix by marking index operand as overwritten for more robust register allocation with SIMD gather instructions.
  • #5589 assures gather operands are distinct by refreshing the index register during instruction selection.
  • #5620 merged a workaround for Sort genvar assertion failures as a stopgap while the proper fix (#5615) is prepared.
  • #5606 reverted inclusion error messages back to "the first"/"the second" phrasing for better alignment with upstream OCaml.
  • Several 5.4 merge PRs were merged: #5618 for boot-compiler build fixes, #5599 for printer conflicts, #5582 for env operation renames, #5504 for lambda conflicts, and #5585 for root file fixes.
  • #5610 was merged, adding [@unpacked] for unboxed product C stub arguments.
  • #5592 fixed a typo in value_kind nullability scraping, correcting subtle level mutation bugs.
  • #5576 refactored the reaper by extracting generic datalog helpers into a separate module.
  • #5452 replaced polymorphic morphism comparisons with explicit equality functions.
  • #5002 added equality functions for CFG basic and terminator instructions as infrastructure for the block merging pass.
entry.summary: A highly active week spanning type system advances, reaper stabilization, backend optimizations, and continued OCaml 5.4 merge work.

entry.title: Week 10, 2026: Intensive backend optimization and type system refinement with 48 merged PRs from 23 contributors
entry.content:
Summary

22 new PRs opened, 48 merged including major testing and infrastructure improvements

Activity

  • #5544 proposes slambda fracturing implementation for static evaluation by Jack Rickard
  • #5567 fixes AVX2 gather instructions incorrectly clearing mask registers by Max Slater
  • #5517 extends abstract kinds to support declarations in recursive modules by Chris Casinghino
  • #5559 changes first parameter of indices to valueornull by Ryan Tjoa
  • Xavier Clerc opened a series on CFG terminator simplification: #5545 (integers), #5546 (floats), #5562 (predecessors)
  • Xavier Clerc opened a series on CFG terminator simplification: #5545 (integers), #5546 (floats), #5562 (predecessors)
  • Merged 48 pull requests covering backend optimization, type system fixes, and infrastructure
  • Continued 5.4 merge conflict resolution: #5557 resolved .mli conflicts, #5426 resolved bytecomp, #5503 resolved tools/ by David Allsopp and Simon Spies
  • Closed #5351 regarding support for additional unboxed datatypes (float16, bfloat16, etc.)
entry.summary: Intensive backend optimization and type system refinement with 48 merged PRs from 23 contributors

entry.title: Week 9, 2026: 15 PRs merged with compiler backend optimizations, type system improvements, and extensive test coverage expansion.
entry.content:
Summary

Major focus on OCaml 5.4 merge preparation, backend testing, and type system development across 8 merged and 11 open PRs.

Activity

  • 5.4 merge conflict resolution: Multiple PRs merged to resolve conflicts including #5477 (driver by David Allsopp), #5469 (file formats by Simon Spies), #5463 (middle/backend by Simon Spies), #5442 (utils by Simon Spies), and #5491 (CI workflow by David Allsopp), with additional PRs in progress for .mli files, parsing, lambda, and bytecomp.
  • Comprehensive backend test coverage (#5447) by @ttebbi-janestreet added 3,300+ lines of [%%expect_asm] tests covering allocation, arrays, floatu, int64u, intrinsics, loops, register allocation, and variants to catch codegen regressions.
  • Old pacing policy removal (#5497) by Nick Barnes cleaned up obsolete GC pacing code after new policy proved effective.
  • Parser-change nag improvements: Multiple fixes by James Rayman including #5507 and #5501 to improve developer workflow when parser changes require ppxlib updates.
  • Magic number bump (#5500) by James Rayman for release 5.2.0minus-32.
  • Layout polymorphism work: Multiple PRs including canonical value descriptions (#5498) and type checking (#5513) by Zesen Qian advancing the sort_/layout_ polymorphism feature.
  • Runtime metaprogramming: Beta-reducing eval in type expressions (#5402) and quote/splice typing fixes (#5323) by Jakub Bachurski.
  • Zero-alloc on function parameters (#5493) by Andrej Ivašković enabling zero_alloc attributes on higher-order function arguments.
  • Mode polymorphism printing (#5488) by @ainageorges implementing comprehensive printing of polymorphic mode variables.
  • Better mode hint formatting (#5516) by Richard Eisenberg improving error message formatting for mode errors.
  • Backend optimizations in review: Register spill/reload improvements (#5514) and codegen tests for intrinsics (#5489) by @ttebbi-janestreet.
  • More Bigarray locality support (#5505) by Vesa Karvonen adding @local_opt annotations to sub, changelayout, and sizein_bytes.
entry.summary: 15 PRs merged with compiler backend optimizations, type system improvements, and extensive test coverage expansion.

entry.title: Week 8, 2026: Active week with type system evolution, backend optimizations, and major Chamelon dune integration work
entry.content:
Summary

Type system enhancements including kind any support, morphisms work, backend optimizations, and 5.4 merge activities

Activity

  • New type system capabilities with Luke Maurer opening PR to allow fields and constructor args of kind any (#5461), changing option parameter kind to any (#5462), and @ainageorges opening PRs for normal form morphisms (#5453) and explicit comparison functions for morphisms (#5452)
  • Backend testing infrastructure expanded by @ttebbi-janestreet with 3,000+ lines of [%%expect_asm] tests for bad codegen cases (#5447), following merged support for expect_asm in tests (#5350)
  • Static computations framework added by Jack Rickard with 1,200+ line implementation affecting 51 files (#5430)
  • Upstream 5.4 merge work progressing through Simon Spies with conflicts resolved in utils (#5442), separation of middle/backend PR diffs (#5460), and resolution of middle/backend conflicts (#5463)
  • Runtime improvements with Nick Barnes opening PRs for ephemeron marking fix (#5450) and sizeclasses.h dependency (#5455), plus Jakub Bachurski fixing ocamldebug's debug_printers and adding CI test-runs (#5465)
  • Chamelon dune integration merged by Basile Clément with nearly 2,000 line addition across 19 files (#5423)
  • Backend spill optimization completed by Xavier Clerc fixing useless spills across 37 files (#5429) with reproduction case added (#5432)
  • Type system refinements merged including redundant modality warning by @dkalinichenko-js affecting 22 files (#5373), pattern variable refactoring to inline records by Zesen Qian (#5437), meet/join terminology regularization by @ainageorges (#5451), and variable pair renaming from 0-indexed to 1-indexed (#5445)
  • Unboxed types bug fixes with Ryan Tjoa fixing bytecode deep copying of unboxed product fields (#5386) and Max Slater preventing effects duplication in %makearray_dynamic (#5459)
  • Compiler infrastructure improvements including removal of thread-local dynamic bindings support by Aspen Smith (#5171), Data Flow lifted constants internalization by Guillaume Bury (#5368), and multiple documentation/tooling fixes by James Rayman (#5468, #5467, #5448, #5435, #5434, #5433)
  • Bug fixes delivered by Simon Spies for coerce typing error printing (#5457), Liam Stevenson for verbose jkind printing (#5449), @ainageorges for comonadictomonadic_X (#5446), Vincent Laviron for degraded value slots types (#5427), and Nick Barnes removing unnecessary runtime call (#5454)
  • Performance optimizations including Zesen Qian optimizing Sort.set to avoid closure allocation (#5443), Richard Eisenberg short-circuiting common case in instancepolyfor_jkind (#5406), and Aspen Smith ensuring proper interrupt initialization (#5424)
  • Fexpr parser work by Keryan Didier removing indexed menhir/ocamllex files (#5440) and adding support for small int and naked floats blocks (#5441)
  • Ongoing draft work includes James Rayman making Stdlib mostly stateless (#5458), Vincent Laviron lowering inlined always attribute priority (#5439), @dkalinichenko-js implementing redundant modifier warning (#5436), and Guillaume Bury fixing Debuginfo.to_location (#5444)
  • Test infrastructure enhanced by Jakub Bachurski with directive for marking test environment as top-level for staging checks (#5464)
entry.summary: Active week with type system evolution, backend optimizations, and major Chamelon dune integration work

entry.title: Week 7, 2026: Active week with 34 merged PRs spanning SIMD/arm64 improvements, abstract kinds, chamelon tooling enhancements, runtime fiber/GC fixes, and build system updates.
entry.content:
Summary

Newly opened PRs include dune integration for chamelon, preemption/tick-thread work, upstream 5.4 merge conflict resolution, and several type-system extensions.

Activity

  • New PRs opened this week include chamelon dune integration by Basile Clément (#5423), framework for static computations in the compiler by Jack Rickard (#5430), type-level eval in type expressions by Jakub Bachurski (#5402), redundant modality warning by @dkalinichenko-js (#5373), and .mli conflict resolution for the 5.4 merge by Simon Spies (#5426)

  • Ongoing preemption work by Aspen Smith continues with the initial preemption implementation (#4881) and tick thread refactoring (#5349); a race fix for requested_external_interrupt initialization was merged (#5424)

  • Upstream OCaml 5.4 merge progresses with a large draft PR covering the full merge (#5325); build system conflicts were resolved (#5376) and missing dynlink prerequisites merged (#5403); a backport emancipating dynlink from compilerlibs is open for review (#5420)

  • CFG backend invariants expanded across the week with terminator arity checks (#5388), basic instruction arity checks (#5390), liveness-at-entry checks (#5375), destruction-point synchronization (#5182), and a new arm64 CI job (#5411), all by Xavier Clerc

  • Mode/modality type system work includes functors affected by modalities (#5371), modules must be aliased many (#5364), warn on imprecise kind annotations (#5362), store jkinds in typedast (#5417), and Sort.set interface simplification (#5419) by @dkalinichenko-js and Zesen Qian

  • DWARF/debugging work has multiple open PRs: CFI information tests revealing existing bugs in amd64.S (#5409), always-emit compilation unit DIE (#5412), --fdebug-prefix-path assembler flag support (#5418), function sections with DWARF (#4934), and example with captured variables debug info (#5422) by Simon Spies and Stephen Dolan

  • chamelon minimizer saw three merged PRs this week: module-based minimization representation (#5356), subcommands (#5421), and build system fix (#5401) by Basile Clément

  • Mode hint system applied to modules for better error messages when mode checking fails (#5034), and functor mode tests updated to use locality/visibility/statefulness axes (#5311) by Zesen Qian and @dkalinichenko-js

  • IRC register allocator now has bit matrix CI coverage; the BIT_MATRIX_THRESHOLD regalloc parameter is now tested in the CI pipeline (#5414) by Xavier Clerc

  • ocamlfilt demangler in pure OCaml continues review (#5100) by Tim McGilchrist and @shym, with discussion about whether to keep or drop the code pointer suffix in demangled names

  • Signal handler interop PR (#5044) by Nick Barnes for Signal_external type addition remains open, with a discussion on signal chaining as an alternative approach

  • Dead exception handler bug reported as issue #5408 by Vincent Laviron: the Flambda2 Simplify pass fails to remove exception handlers (and associated traps) when the local exception optimisation triggers
entry.summary: Active week with 34 merged PRs spanning SIMD/arm64 improvements, abstract kinds, chamelon tooling enhancements, runtime fiber/GC fixes, and build system updates.

entry.title: Week 6, 2026: Type system additions with bool# primitives, borrowing support, and new Format_doc API
entry.content:
Summary

21 new PRs opened including functors modality work, manual initialization, and bytecode product fields

Activity

  • 21 new PRs opened this week including @dkalinichenko-js work on functors with modalities (#5371), Mark Shinwell manual module initialization (#5395), Ryan Tjoa bytecode deep copy of product fields (#5386), James Rayman small int ctz/clz/popcnt (#5393), @ttebbi-janestreet expect_asm support (#5350), and Zesen Qian parsing for let poly_ and val poly_ syntax (#5392)
  • Upstream backports progressed with Simon Spies merging Formatdoc introduction (#5357) plus opening three additional Formatdoc refinement PRs (#5378, #5380, #5381) and build system conflict resolution (#5376)
  • Flambda2 reaper improvements by Nathanaëlle Courant with exception handler parameter fix preventing incorrect unboxing (#5377), overzealous rebuild check fix (#5374), and dead code removal (#5379)
  • CFG backend work by Xavier Clerc merging simplify terminator bug fix (#5389) and opening liveness checking at function entry (#5375), basic invariant validation (#5390), and additional arity tests for terminators (#5388)
  • Match-in-match optimization fix by Guillaume Bury for over-applications in specialized handlers merged after 8+ weeks (#5119), with follow-up flow analysis cleanup (#5370) and ongoing data flow constant handling (#5368)
  • Chamelon build tool enhanced by Basile Clément with consistent ocamlformat config merged (#5387), module minimization in progress (#5356), and reaper constant type rewriting opened (#5391)
  • Type system and modalities work by @dkalinichenko-js including internal ticket 5116 fix merged (#5372), redundant modality warning (#5373), imprecise kind annotation warnings (#5362), and modules aliased many requirement (#5364)
  • Dissector fixes by Mark Shinwell removing overzealous AS check (#5396), ehframehdr section removal (#5394), and eh_frame cleanup in form rewrite (#5384)
  • Runtime and tooling improvements including Max Slater fiber return to allocating domain (#5363) and ARM test enablement (#5385), Zesen Qian printtyp modalities exception fix (#5382), @tkoukpari local annotations (#5366), and cleanup of AI agent make output (#5365) plus test environment scrubbing (#5361)
  • Build and configuration updates including opam switch configuration (#5360), prefetching feature detection macro fixes (#5359), and Jekyll front matter for let mutable docs (#5341)
  • Kind system enhancements with non-value kinds crossing externality (#5162), bigstring safety property refactoring (#5307), and removal of Unknown_non_rigid (#5335)
  • Address review skill tool implementation merged providing automated PR review response workflow (#5367)
  • Classic mode inlining fallback heuristic disabled to improve optimization behavior in non-Flambda2 mode (#5383)
entry.summary: Type system additions with bool# primitives, borrowing support, and new Format_doc API

entry.title: Week 5, 2026: ARM64 binary emitter shipped with verification infrastructure, plus new unboxed unit type and syntax
entry.content:
Summary

24 new PRs opened including type system work, backend improvements, and upstream merge efforts

Activity

  • Jakub Bachurski opened six new PRs for metaprogramming improvements including module pack annotations (#5320), quote and splice typing fixes (#5323), lazy substitution construction (#5347), empty Tpoly handling (#5336), test installation paths (#5314), and revert consideration (#5317)
  • Ryan Tjoa opened four new PRs advancing kind system with box_ type operator (#5342), box_kind constructor (#5343), block index optimization (#5328), and [@flatten_floats] attribute requirement (#5329)
  • David Allsopp opened two infrastructure PRs for tool version checking in configure (#5348) and simpler development opam switch management (#5344)
  • Simon Spies opened upstream merge PR for OCaml 5.4 integration (#5325) and runtime array function rename backport (#5339)
  • Luke Maurer opened PR allowing fields and constructor arguments of kind any (#5330)
  • Aspen Smith opened draft PR moving tick thread into runtime for effects system (#5349)
  • Zesen Qian opened PR removing Unknown_non_rigid from type system (#5335) and merged refactoring combining here_occurs and access_order into unified usage_order type (#5302)
  • Vesa Karvonen opened draft PR making Reg_class the source of physical register information (#5308) and merged build dependency fix (#5318)
  • Xavier Clerc opened draft PR changing linear instruction representation to DLL.t (#5331) and merged zero-alloc exception handling fix (#5179)
  • Max Slater opened PR refactoring bigstring get/set safety properties for SIMD primitives (#5307)
  • @dkalinichenko-js opened draft PR for functor mode test updates (#5311) and merged test disablement (#5327) plus signature modality improvements (#5290)
  • James Rayman opened PR adding Jekyll front matter to let mutable docs (#5341)
  • Keryan Didier opened PR for flambda files in ocamltest (#5346)
  • Jacob Van Buren opened magic number bump PR (#5332) and merged instruction ordering consistency fix (#5334)
  • Build and CI improvements merged including toplevel .depend file removal by Simon Spies (#5333), test installation fixes by Jakub Bachurski (#5316), macOS test enablement (#5337), and merge_group CI triggers (#5321, #5322)
  • Type system refinements merged including Merlin jkind printing by Liam Stevenson (#5304), functor modality zapping by Zesen Qian (#5306), and Typedtree mode fixes by Jakub Bachurski (#5319)
  • Backend architecture work including dissector partition improvements (#5310), Cfg.Raise argument fixes (#5315, #5313), and compilation unit path handling by @shym (#5289)
entry.summary: ARM64 binary emitter shipped with verification infrastructure, plus new unboxed unit type and syntax

entry.title: Week 4, 2026: Flambda2 optimization work, ARM64 improvements, modes/modalities for Merlin, and testing infrastructure enhancements
entry.content:
Summary

Type system work on modes/modalities/borrowing, Flambda2 improvements, ARM64 SIMD vectors, and backend refactoring

Activity

  • Nine new PRs opened including improved Merlin jkind printing by Liam Stevenson (#5304), 64-bit .eh_frame support for dissector by Mark Shinwell (#5303), refactored uniqueness analysis errors by Zesen Qian (#5302), small int conversions by Richard Eisenberg (#5305), ARM wide vector translation by Max Slater (#5291), signature default modalities per axis by @dkalinichenko-js (#5290), and DragonFly BSD build fixes (WIP, breaks Linux) by Michael Neumann (#5282)
  • Modes and modalities work advanced through multiple merged PRs by Liam Stevenson adding mode/modality locations to typedtree for Merlin syntax hovers (#5218), testing mode annotations make it through to typedtree (#5297), and exposing actual mode in Texp_ident for variable reference tooltips (#5300)
  • Borrowing support PR from Zesen Qian remains active with ongoing discussion about error messages for out-of-context borrowing and implementation scope, representing simplified uniqueness via borrow_ operator for temporary aliased access (#5215)
  • Flambda2 backend refactoring moved alloc_mode out of call_kind by Nathanaëlle Courant in preparation for zero-alloc changes (#5287), merged Pmakemixedblock into Pmakeblock as minimum required for layout polymorphism by Jack Rickard (#5238), and introduced Compilation_unit.full_path_with_arguments_as_string refactoring by @shym (#5289)
  • ARM64 and SIMD development including wide vector translation to tuples on ARM (vec256 → vec128 & vec128) enabling 256-bit SIMD tests by Max Slater (#5291), arrays of unboxed pairs of vec128s as prerequisite (#5239), binary emitter verification with new -verify-binary-emitter option (#5198), and extra owee functionality with extensive review feedback from ChatGPT 5.1 Codex identifying relocation issues (#5188)
  • Type system infrastructure advanced with shape constructors for unknown types in preparation for improved layouts in debug information by Simon Spies (#4928), instantiated type declaration parameters in Typeopt avoiding caml_modify by @dkalinichenko-js (#5108), and ongoing work on scannable axes for nullability/separability (#5169)
  • Backend and register allocation improvements with IRC interference graph extraction to dedicated module (#5237) and bit matrix representation exploration for performance benchmarking (#5296) by Xavier Clerc
  • Developer tooling updates including Claude AI agent project settings (#5281), sandboxing experimentation (disabled by default due to git worktree issues) (#5284), and build output cleanup that was reverted due to macOS compatibility with cp --remove-destination (#5285, #5301)
  • Ongoing feature development includes new reversible name mangling scheme (#5099), unsigned division primitives (#4936), fexpr primitive representation rework (#5221), type-level eval experiments (#5288), annotating polymorphic applications under quotes (#5154), phantom variable DWARF support (#4595), improved layouts for debug information (#4916), and faster oldify for minor collector performance (#5163)
entry.summary: Flambda2 optimization work, ARM64 improvements, modes/modalities for Merlin, and testing infrastructure enhancements

entry.title: Week 3, 2026: ARM64 backend improvements, mode system refinements, stdlib additions, and testing infrastructure enhancements
entry.content:
Summary

Ongoing work on native expect tests, SIMD vector arrays, layout polymorphism, and long-running mode documentation

Activity

  • Native expect test infrastructure opened by James Rayman with three related PRs: expect.opt for native toplevel testing (#5245), [%%expect.ignore_echo] directive to ignore REPL output (#5244), and spacing consistency between native and bytecode toplevels (#5243)
  • SIMD vector development by Max Slater continues with support for arrays of unboxed vec128 pairs (#5239) and translation of wide vectors to tuples on ARM (#5240)
  • Layout polymorphism work progressing through Jack Rickard merging Pmakemixedblock into Pmakeblock (#5238) and adding slambda constructors (#5148)
  • Mode/modality typedtree annotations under active development by Liam Stevenson with extensive design discussions between contributors about proper type representation (#5218)
  • Xavier Clerc extracted interference graph to standalone module for register allocator reuse (#5237)
  • Runtime improvements include Nick Barnes improving Gc.stat and Gc.quick_stat accuracy for runtime 5 (#5226)
  • Long-running PRs seeing continued activity: modality documentation by Richard Eisenberg (#4074), stdlib stateless annotations (#4839), Lambda.layout usage in transl_exp (#4582), and level preservation in Typeopt.value_kind (#4549)
  • Flambda2 work by Basile Clément and Nathanaëlle Courant addressing reaper issues and inlining heuristics (#5157, #5108)
  • George Shammas working on enabling CI tests in Nix builds (#5228)
entry.summary: ARM64 backend improvements, mode system refinements, stdlib additions, and testing infrastructure enhancements

entry.title: Week 2, 2026: Upgraded to OCaml 5.4.0, added implicit kinds feature, improved Format stdlib with unicode width support, and enhanced mode system
entry.content:
Summary

Active development on borrowing syntax, ARM64 improvements, debug info, and metaprogramming with 18 new PRs opened

Activity

  • Borrowing syntax implementation by Zesen Qian introduces simpler borrow_ syntax for unique references without relying on finer regionality (#5215)
  • ARM64 platform work by Mark Shinwell includes making ocaml-jit platform independent (#5186), typed DSL rewrite (#5193), improved label/symbol typing (#5185), binary emitter experiments (#5198, #5188, #5192), and arm64 logical immediate tests (#5201)
  • Debug information improvements by Simon Spies with increased fidelity levels (#5199), performance enhancements (#5124), named binders for shapes (#5111), layout improvements (#4916), shape constructors for unknown types (#4928), function sections support (#4934), and default shapes format change (#5103)
  • Chamelon bug reducer enhancements by Basile Clément with improved match minimizers (#5212), value binding fixes (#5222), -dfexpr-annot flag for dumping fexpr (#5210), and build system improvements (#5209)
  • Metaprogramming and quotation work includes type inspection annotation handling by Jakub Bachurski for polymorphic applications (#5154) with additional tests (#5214), and translquote review by Jack Rickard (#4981)
  • Type system enhancements with layout-polymorphic types by Andrej Ivašković adding repr_ syntax and AST nodes (#5068), slambda constructors by Jack Rickard (#5148), ikinds implementation by @julesjacobs with new subsumption and mode crossing algorithms (#5159), and typedtree mode/modality location tracking by Liam Stevenson (#5218)
  • Backend improvements include CMM/CFG preservation of Invalid by Vesa Karvonen (#5208), fexpr primitive representation rework by Keryan Didier (#5221), name mangling scheme by Tim McGilchrist for reversible full module paths (#5099), and constant switch optimization tweaks by Vincent Laviron (#5200)
  • Module mode hints ongoing work by Zesen Qian utilizing new hint system for better error messages (#5034)
  • Effects and preemption continued development by Aspen Smith on initial preemption implementation (#4881) and effect primitive refactoring (#4901)
entry.summary: Upgraded to OCaml 5.4.0, added implicit kinds feature, improved Format stdlib with unicode width support, and enhanced mode system

entry.title: Week 1, 2026: Dissector pass for linking large executables merged, arm64 work progressing, modes system improvements underway
entry.content:
Summary

17 new PRs opened including arm64 DSL, modes improvements, and tooling updates; arm64 and modes work progressing

Activity

  • New PRs opened this week include Nix flake update for OCaml 5.4 by Jacob Van Buren (#5194), modality hints by Zesen Qian (#5183), scannable axis modifiers by Ryan Tjoa (#5187), ocamlformat upgrade by Simon Spies (#5180), and multiple arm64 infrastructure PRs by Mark Shinwell including typed DSL (#5193), logical immediate refactoring (#5192), and platform-independent ocaml-jit (#5186)
  • Arm64 binary emitter work progressing with infrastructure improvements including typed DSL rewrite (#5193), improved label/symbol typing in Asm_directives (#5185), extra owee functionality (#5188), and binary emitter itself in draft form (#5177)
  • Modes system refinements with Zesen Qian working on modality hints (#5183), explicit valmodalities application (#5190), and module mode hints (#5034), while Ryan Tjoa addresses scannable axes behavior (#5187, #5169) and ornull layout precision (#5176, #5178)
  • Zero-alloc checker improvements by Xavier Clerc preventing Widen exception escapes (#5179) and synchronizing destruction point checks (#5182)
  • Dissector refinements following merge with stack overflow fix (#5191) and test simplification (#5195) by Simon Spies and Mark Shinwell
  • Metaprogramming features advancing with quote disambiguation work by Jakub Bachurski for records/variants (#5094) and polymorphic applications (#5154)
  • OCaml 5.4 upgrade preparations continuing by Simon Spies with ocamlformat version bump (#5180) as part of broader upgrade effort (#5036)
entry.summary: Dissector pass for linking large executables merged, arm64 work progressing, modes system improvements underway

entry.title: Week 52, 2025: Jkind error reporting improvements, CFG backend bug fixes, and new PRs for `or_null` type handling and ARM64 machine code emitter
entry.content:
Summary

New PRs for or_null type improvements, ARM64 binary emitter, and unboxed keywords; ongoing work on effects, preemption, and layout polymorphism

Activity

  • New or_null type handling work by Ryan Tjoa with PRs addressing imprecise separability in type_jkind (#5178) and improved layout estimation for or_null instances (#5176)
  • ARM64 binary emitter introduced by Mark Shinwell as new machine code emitter with typed DSL for ARM64 architecture, making ocaml-jit library architecture-independent (#5177)
  • Unboxed keywords feature proposed by Nailen Matschke adding #;, #if, #assert, #while, #for, #when, and #<- variants that work with unit# and bool# instead of boxed types (#5167)
  • Quotation system improvements by Jakub Bachurski including infrastructure for type inspection information merged (#5090) and ongoing work on record/variant disambiguation under quotes (#5094)
  • Stage checking for record labels progressing with Andrej Ivašković implementing proper stage checks preventing labels from crossing quotation locks (#5158)
  • Nix installation improvements by George Shammas switching to Cachix install action to avoid download timeouts (#5168)
  • Effects and runtime work includes Aspen Smith implementing preemption support (#4881), refactoring effect primitives (#4901), removing thread-local dynamic bindings (#5171), and implementing Dynamic in stdlib (#5172)
  • Backend and optimization work continuing with Mark Shinwell developing the dissector pass for linking large executables beyond 2GB (#5146), Xavier Clerc fixing ARM64 large stack offsets (#5130), and James Rayman adding unsigned division primitives (#4936)
  • Type system enhancements with @dkalinichenko-js working on implicit kinds (#4285), Nailen Matschke making non-value base kinds cross externality by default (#5162), and Ryan Tjoa preparing scannable axes implementation in draft PR (#5169)
  • Layout polymorphism infrastructure advancing with Jack Rickard adding slambda constructors for static evaluation support (#5148) and Zesen Qian enabling functors with non-legacy mode input/output (#5107)
entry.summary: Jkind error reporting improvements, CFG backend bug fixes, and new PRs for `or_null` type handling and ARM64 machine code emitter

entry.title: Week 51, 2025: Compiler development focused on reaper bug fixes, type system improvements, and layout polymorphism work
entry.content:
Summary

23 new PRs opened including layout poly work, dissector prototype, and bool# type; 26 PRs merged focusing on reaper fixes

Activity

  • New work opened this week includes performance improvements for debug info generation by Simon Spies (#5124), arm64 large stack offset fixes by Xavier Clerc (#5130), SSE register class addition by Aspen Smith (#5136), dissector prototype for linking large executables with small code model by Mark Shinwell (#5146), slambda constructors by Jack Rickard (#5148), abstract unit# type with void kind by Ryan Tjoa (#5151), bool# type with unboxed literals by Nailen Matschke (#5166), and scannable axes for nullability computation by Ryan Tjoa (#5169, #5170)
  • Reaper development reached stability milestone with Nathanaëlle Courant fixing multiple crashes including unused argument unboxing (#5129), resulttypes parameter ordering (#5137), indirectunknownarity argument deletion (#5145), and resulttypes rewriting (#5125), plus Basile Clément adding CI configuration (#5149) and fixing switch operation counting (#5144)
  • Type system enhancements delivered through depth variable rewriting in coercions by Basile Clément (#5134), Is_int and Get_tag pattern support (#5140), mod bounds debug printing fix by Zack Eisbach (#5164), and bigarray type index annotations (#5135)
  • Compiler infrastructure work included abstract kinds refactoring eliminating cyclic dependencies by Chris Casinghino (#5070), magic number bump for 5.2.0minus-25 release (#5127), CI timeout fixes with cachix action (#5168), debug information gathering improvements (#5096), and stack slot tracking in counters (#5132)
  • Optimization improvements merged including consideration of value arguments as non-useful for inlining (#5093), jkindtypes refactoring with getsort moved (#5152), and scannable axes influencing Lambda translation (#5155)
entry.summary: Compiler development focused on reaper bug fixes, type system improvements, and layout polymorphism work

entry.title: Week 50, 2025: Untagged int arrays landed alongside compiler optimization fixes and type system refinements
entry.content:
Summary

Type system work focused on abstract kinds, modes, and layouts alongside ongoing debug info and name mangling improvements

Activity

  • New PRs opened include reversible name mangling by Tim McGilchrist enabling full module path reconstruction (#5099), match-in-match fix for over-applications by Guillaume Bury (#5119), functor non-legacy mode support by Zesen Qian (#5107), debug info performance improvements by Simon Spies (#5124), and IRC affinity tweaks by Xavier Clerc (#5113)
  • Type system refactoring merged abstract kinds part 1 by Chris Casinghino eliminating cyclic dependencies to enable abstract kinds (#5070), with part 2 adding basic abstract kinds and kind aliases now under review (#5071)
  • Layout and axis work includes Zack Eisbach merging nullability as scannable axis (#5086), adding non_pointer64 to separability axis (#5095), and opening PR to use scannable axes in Lambda translation for avoiding unnecessary caml_modify calls (#5104)
  • Debug information infrastructure progressing with Tim McGilchrist opening PRs for gathering debug info (#5096), improved debug tracking (#5098), and name mangling configuration (#5097), while Simon Spies works on named binders for shapes (#5111) and shape default flipping (#5103)
  • Ongoing work includes preemption by Aspen Smith (#4881), translquote review by Jack Rickard (#4981), untagged int indexing primitives by Jacob Van Buren (#4779), repr_ syntax additions by Jakub Bachurski (#5068), and spawnable mode by Ryan Tjoa (#5110)
  • Quote-related fixes progressing with Andrej Ivašković addressing annotation drops on let rec (#5083), elaboration of compound terms (#5090), and record/variant disambiguation (#5094)
  • Infrastructure improvements merged musl variant for GitHub CI by Kevin Svetlitski (#5123) and Reaper debugging flag by Nathanaëlle Courant (#5106)
entry.summary: Untagged int arrays landed alongside compiler optimization fixes and type system refinements

entry.title: Week 49, 2025: Unboxed arrays, flambda2 reaper enhancements, AVX2 SIMD support, and abstract kinds foundation work progressing
entry.content:
Summary

Abstract kinds foundation (2 large PRs under review), name mangling series (5 PRs), nullability/separability axis work continuing

Activity

  • Abstract kinds implementation proceeding through multi-PR series with #5070 refactoring to eliminate cyclic dependencies (1858 additions, 1498 deletions across 13 files) and #5071 adding basic abstract kinds and kind aliases (3241 additions, 1033 deletions, 103 files) both opened and under active review by Liam Stevenson and Richard Eisenberg
  • Name mangling overhaul advancing with five related PRs from Tim McGilchrist: reversible name mangling scheme (#5099), ocamlfilt demangler implementation (#5100), configuration options (#5097), improved debug tracking (#5098), and debug info gathering (#5096)
  • Nullability and separability work continuing with Zack Eisbach opening #5095 adding non_pointer64 on separability axis, #5086 making nullability scannable, and #5104 using scannable axes to influence Lambda translation
  • Reaper enhancements merged with #5081 adding auto mode for direct call preservation and #5043 implementing type rewriting in the reaper by Nathanaëlle Courant
  • Flambda2 indirect calls improved through #4809 adding set of possible code_ids to Indirect_known_arity call kind, enabling better optimization of indirect calls with known arity by Nathanaëlle Courant
  • Backend register allocation refactored with #4888 making Reg.t private by Greta Yorsh in preparation for register aliases with accurate types
  • Quotation improvements progressing with Jakub Bachurski working on disambiguation (#5094), elaboration framework (#5090), test organization (#5089), and annotation fixes (#5083)
  • Datalog provenance enhanced by Basile Clément with #5091 allowing dynamic enabling at rule evaluation time rather than module load time
  • Unboxed arrays expanded with James Rayman continuing work on #4643 for int8#, int16#, and int# arrays (3677 additions, 70 files)
  • Register allocation affinity under development in #5059 by Xavier Clerc implementing basic affinity support for all three allocators
  • Flambda2 inlining refined through #5093 by Basile Clément considering value arguments non-useful for speculative inlining decisions
  • CFG dataflow refactored in #5045 by Greta Yorsh unifying forward and backward analysis types
  • Documentation efforts advancing with Basile Clément creating encyclopædia of flambda2 optimizations in #5082
  • Local mode pointers being debugged in #5087 by Ryan Tjoa fixing segfault with %unsafe_set_ptr and NULL base
  • Shapes format under consideration in #5103 by Simon Spies for flipping default format
  • ASAN runtime optimization proposed in #5102 by Kevin Svetlitski to disable instrumentation in compiler's own runtime, reducing compilation overhead from 40-45% to 20-25%
entry.summary: Unboxed arrays, flambda2 reaper enhancements, AVX2 SIMD support, and abstract kinds foundation work progressing

entry.title: Week 48, 2025: Backend optimization surge with 10 x86 peephole PRs, type system improvements, and new intrinsics support
entry.content:
Summary

26 new PRs opened including abstract kinds preparation, typed ARM64 DSL, and separability axis work; backend optimization continues

Activity

  • New PRs opened this week include abstract kinds preparation by Chris Casinghino (#5070), typed ARM64 DSL by Mark Shinwell (#5037), separability axis scannable conversion by Zack Eisbach (#5031), register allocation affinity support by Xavier Clerc (#5059), and x86 peephole optimizations continuation (#5063)
  • Backend optimization surge led by Xavier Clerc with 10 merged PRs implementing x86 peephole rules for redundant comparison removal (#5028), MOV-to-LEA rewriting (#5018), MOV-to-XCHG optimization (#5016), useless move deletion (#4985), MOV chain optimization (#4977), infrastructure refactoring (#5032, #4975), CI testing (#4976), and IRC refactoring (#4594)
  • Type system evolution with Liam Stevenson optimizing jkind normalization fuel (#5047, #5009) and environment fold operations (#5066), James Rayman re-enabling unboxed module elements (#5064), Zesen Qian adding staticity checking (#5054) and mode hints (#5053), and Vincent Laviron fixing classic mode approximations (#5058)
  • Intrinsics expansion by Max Slater adding 128-bit integer operations (#5025) and BMI/BMI2 support (#5065)
  • Flambda2 cleanup with Basile Clément removing 3-year-old disabled code age relation join (#5051) and improving chamelon (#5061), Nathanaëlle Courant refactoring reaper code (#5049, #5038)
  • Ongoing work includes reaper type rewriting (#5043), GADT with-bounds re-enablement (#5046), typed ARM64 DSL (#5037), flambda2 type interface (#5050), debugging improvements by Simon Spies (#4916, #4961, #4928), name mangling scheme by Tim McGilchrist (#4850), and untagged int arrays by James Rayman (#4779, #4643, #4936)
  • Developer tooling improvements including manifest files by Andrei Odintsov (#4986), magic number bump by @dkalinichenko-js (#5048), boot directory removal by Jacob Van Buren (#5067), test fix by Vesa Karvonen (#5042), and documentation typo fix by BuzzY_ (#5069)
entry.summary: Backend optimization surge with 10 x86 peephole PRs, type system improvements, and new intrinsics support

entry.title: Week 47, 2025: Mixed module support, type system improvements, runtime optimizations, backend enhancements, and probes implementation
entry.content:
Summary

Active development across type system, backend optimizations, runtime improvements, and open PRs for SIMD and layouts

Activity

  • Newly opened PRs include separability as scannable axis renaming pointerness (#5031), fuel optimization with linear consumption (#5009), AVX2 gather intrinsics support (#5040), 128-bit integer arithmetic intrinsics (#5025), staticity mode checking (#5029), immutable arrays of unboxed products (#5026), typed DSL for arm64 (#5037), and module mode hint system (#5034)
  • Type system improvements delivered through Tof_jkind for unboxed existentials and Tpolys by Liam Stevenson (#4995), v1 safety check optimization avoiding substitutions (#5039), and upstream PR 13866 with marking mechanism updates (#5015)
  • Backend enhancements include probes code generation refactoring by Greta Yorsh (#5011), CFG profiling counters for instructions, blocks, and moves by Xavier Clerc (#5027), and CFG terminator simplification reinstated with reducibility check (#4921)
  • Runtime optimizations landed via marking loop optimization removing global access by Nick Barnes (#4997), sweep loop global variable elimination (#5022), and multidomain testing without stack-checks by Vesa Karvonen (#5019)
  • Infrastructure updates merged including metrics collection refactor for per-artifact tracking by Xavier Clerc (#5003), compiler profile data archiving (#5004), Dune version bump to 3.17.2 by Sadiq Jaffer (#5000), and Chamelon boot compiler compatibility by Basile Clément (#5020)
  • Active development continues on .cmxs format optimization with consolidated imports (#5014), slambda splices integration (#5023), arm64 intrinsics for RDTSC and CRC32 (#5024), reaper consistency for apply (#5038), dump-dir race condition fix (#5010), and manifest files support (#4986)
entry.summary: Mixed module support, type system improvements, runtime optimizations, backend enhancements, and probes implementation

entry.title: Week 46, 2025: Modes system enhancements, SIMD intrinsics expansion, slambda IR introduction, and metaprogramming improvements
entry.content:
Summary

Twenty-seven new PRs opened covering pointerness axes, jkind normalization, probes, metaprogramming, and GC optimizations

Activity

  • New PRs opened include downstream of ocaml/ocaml#13866 for occur_rec optimization by Liam Stevenson (#5015), unboxed module elements allowing non-value fields by James Rayman (#4020), Tof_jkind for unboxed existentials by Liam Stevenson (#4995), pointerness as scannable axis by Zack Eisbach (#5006), runtime metaprogramming extension by Andrej Ivašković (#5012), and fuel increase to 10 with linear normalization by Liam Stevenson (#5009)
  • Backend and infrastructure work progressing with .cmxs format changes for import headers by Simon Spies (#5014), debug info ordering fix between Name_for_debugger and stack spilling (#5013), unoptimized probes implementation by Mark Shinwell (#4991) and Greta Yorsh (#5007), probes refactoring (#5011), compile-time arch constants (#4972), and x86 peephole optimization for dead register moves by Xavier Clerc (#4985)
  • Metrics and CI improvements delivered through per-artifact size tracking instead of aggregation by Xavier Clerc (#5003), profile collection alongside artifact sizes (#5004), CFG instruction equality functions (#5002), and -dump-dir race condition fix (#5010)
  • Type system and modes development advancing with metaprogramming review by Jack Rickard (#4981), arrays of GC-ignorable values by @dkalinichenko-js (#4963), crossing parsing support by Zack Eisbach (#4781), limited mod contended inference (#4929), and Typeopt.value_kind level preservation (#4549)
  • Debugging infrastructure work including LLDB OxCaml language plugin by Simon Spies (#4961), function sections with DWARF support (#4934), and reversible name mangling scheme by Tim McGilchrist (#4850)
  • Runtime and GC optimization explored through effect primitives refactoring removing Must_not_enter_gc by Aspen Smith (#4901), preemption implementation (#4881), and major GC marking loop optimization by Nick Barnes (#4997)
  • Miscellaneous improvements including manifest files support by Andrei Odintsov (#4986), dune version documentation update by Sadiq Jaffer (#5000), and Ccatch simplification for boolean computation by Xavier Clerc (#4998)
entry.summary: Modes system enhancements, SIMD intrinsics expansion, slambda IR introduction, and metaprogramming improvements

entry.title: Week 45, 2025: Significant progress on modes, SIMD support, and Unix module annotations with 34 PRs merged and 17 new PRs opened.
entry.content:
Summary

17 new PRs opened including SIMD enhancements and peephole optimizations, with 34 PRs merged covering modes, quotations, and tooling.

Activity

  • New PRs opened this week include #4987 and #4988 for SIMD improvements (stop sign-extending small int casts, generalize memory operands), #4982 for a new sharable mode, #4983 to move share_context to mode hints, and several WIP PRs from Xavier Clerc on x86 peephole optimizations (#4973, #4975, #4977, #4985)
  • Mode system improvements were a major theme with Zesen Qian contributing #4792 (convert mode to constant in cmi), #4984 (disallow legacy mode syntax in comprehension), #4968 (compiler no longer prints old mode/modality syntax), #4979 (remove excessive parentheses in mode error hints), and #4978 (add missing Skip hints for monadic modes)
  • Quotations and splicing saw fixes with Jakub Bachurski landing #4974 for inclusion checks with quotes and splices, Leo White fixing quotation unification in #4906, and Zesen Qian fixing constructor quotation locks in #4910
  • Compiler internals improved with Liam Stevenson making module finding lazy in #4970, Mark Shinwell removing global state from Linkenv in #4947, and Luke Maurer fixing a segfault by disallowing aliases to parameter modules in #4948
  • Type system and jkinds work included Andrej Ivašković adding levels to sort variables in #4785, Dmitrii Kalinichenko fixing or_null with default optional arguments in #4953 and landing #4891 for exceptions crossing statefulness and visibility
  • Documentation and tooling updates included Max Slater fixing tutorial typos in #4954 and #4969, Zesen Qian rewording allocation hints in #4957, and Xavier Clerc restoring oxcaml-metrics push in #4967
entry.summary: Significant progress on modes, SIMD support, and Unix module annotations with 34 PRs merged and 17 new PRs opened.

entry.title: Week 44, 2025: Thread naming support, Flambda2 type system improvements, CI infrastructure upgrades, and DWARF debugging enhancements
entry.content:
Summary

Flambda2 type system work, modality system improvements, DWARF debugging advances, and parameterized modules refinements

Activity

  • Flambda2 type system enhancement with relations stored as projections in type kinds, enabling precise tracking during n-way joins for the match-in-match heuristic by Basile Clément with extensive review from Vincent Laviron (#4390)
  • Profiling infrastructure for speculative inlining added by Basile Clément to better understand compilation behavior (#4375)
  • Domain index exposure proposed by Vesa Karvonen adding Domain.self_index API for per-domain array elements to avoid contention, referencing upstream ocaml/ocaml#13171 (#4950)
  • Linkenv refactoring by Mark Shinwell removing global state to simplify code and support future work, under review by Jack Rickard (#4947)
  • Natdynlink performance improvement using skiplist instead of linked list for module GC roots by Nicolas Trangez, enabling efficient duplicate checking during dynamic linking (#4949)
  • Parameterized modules segfault fix disallowing aliases to parameter modules to prevent signature substitution issues until transparent ascription is available (#4948)
  • DWARF debugging advances including layout improvements for runtime type information by Simon Spies (#4916), shape constructors for unknown types (#4928), function sections support (#4934), and phantom variable support by Mark Shinwell (#4595)
  • Modality system work with @@ global implying @@ aliased for borrowing soundness by @dkalinichenko-js (#4889), mod bound parsing refactoring (#4931), mod contended inference (#4929, #4944), exceptions crossing statefulness and visibility (#4891), and stdlib stateless annotations (#4839, #4930)
  • Stdlib local annotations for printing functions by @tkoukpari across multiple iterations (#4937, #4938, #4943)
  • Mixed modules progress with automatic field reordering by James Rayman (#4020) and unsigned divmod work (#4936)
  • Layout system improvements using Lambda.layout for transl_exp and preserving levels in Typeopt.value_kind by @dkalinichenko-js (#4582, #4549)
  • Type system testing adding jkind subsumption across files test by Aspen Smith with review from Liam Stevenson (#4922)
  • Backend optimizations including CFG peephole rules for neutral elements (#4932), float value propagation (#4879), terminator simplification extensions (#4807), and CFG reducibility work by Xavier Clerc (#4920, #4921)
  • Name mangling scheme development by Tim McGilchrist enabling reversible demangling with full module paths for improved debugging (#4850)
  • Preemption support ongoing work by Aspen Smith (#4881)
  • Runtime improvements including memprof configuration on major heap by Stephen Dolan (#4939) and to_cmm illegal reordering prevention by Guillaume Bury (#4941)
  • CI metrics enhancement for compiler profile data collection by Xavier Clerc (#4942)
  • Frametables configuration option for .lrodata placement to handle large code sizes by Mark Shinwell (#4905)
  • Match-in-match heuristic development with improved naked immediate meets by Basile Clément (#4374, #4394)
entry.summary: Thread naming support, Flambda2 type system improvements, CI infrastructure upgrades, and DWARF debugging enhancements

entry.title: Week 43, 2025: Runtime improvements for threading and GC control, mode system enhancements, and compiler bug fixes
entry.content:
Summary

Runtime threading improvements, mode system refinements, and ongoing work on preemption, name mangling, and parsing features

Activity

  • Preemption support under development with 2500+ line draft PR affecting 62 files for concurrent execution control by Aspen Smith (#4881)
  • Name mangling scheme in progress with reversible mangling for full module path reconstruction and demangling implementations for LLVM, Linux kernel, and binutils by Tim McGilchrist (#4850)
  • Crossing syntax parsing adding mod support for per-value mode tracking with 1100+ line implementation by Zack Eisbach (#4781)
  • Mixed modules with non-values allowing automatic reordering of module fields to support unboxed types across module boundaries by James Rayman (#4020)
  • Effect primitive refactoring to remove Must_not_enter_gc module by making %resume take continuation directly instead of unpacked fields by Aspen Smith (#4901)
  • Backup thread locking improvements extending caml_locking_scheme interface with better backup thread interactions for custom locking schemes by Stephen Dolan (#4914)
  • Quotation system fixes addressing unification bugs in quote/splice handling for metaprogramming by Leo White (#4906, #4911, #4912)
  • Constructor quotation locks bug fixing predef type checking to properly handle locally-defined constructors by Zesen Qian (#4910)
  • Frametable relocation optimization proposing .lrodata section placement to reduce text section size and prevent linker failures under default code model by Mark Shinwell (#4905)
  • CFG reducibility checking and related terminator simplification improvements for backend optimization by Xavier Clerc (#4920, #4921, #4807, #4879)
  • Untagged int arrays implementation adding int8#, int16#, and int# array support with proper tag selection and indexing by James Rayman (#4643)
  • jsofocaml integration comprehensive test suite and CI updates for JavaScript backend testing by Jacob Van Buren (#4753)
  • CI runner upgrades enabling larger build machines for faster compilation and test execution by Jacob Van Buren (#4908)
  • LLVM backend flag control adding -no-llvm-backend option for selective testing by Greta Yorsh (#4904)
  • Primitive list generation tool discussion about auto-generating comprehensive primitive documentation by James Rayman (#4812)
  • Debug information layouts improving runtime type representation for flattened arrays by Simon Spies (#4916)
  • Slambda IR development introducing static evaluation phase between translation and lambda with trivial initial implementation by Luke Maurer (#4776)
  • Mode system refinements including hints for containing types, global/aliased implications, and Lambda.layout usage by @dkalinichenko-js (#4919, #4889, #4582, #4549)
  • Jkind subsumption test reproducing cross-file bug for investigation by Aspen Smith (#4922)
  • Ikind system prototype implementing new subsumption and mode crossing algorithms behind -extension ikinds flag by @julesjacobs (#4675)
  • VERSION file cleanup removing installation dependency to match upstream OCaml by @dkalinichenko-js (#4924)
  • Typeopt level preservation fixing unintended type variable level changes affecting principal typing and nullability by @dkalinichenko-js (#4549)
  • Lex directory removal cleaning up unused lexer code and dependent tests by James Rayman (#4925)
entry.summary: Runtime improvements for threading and GC control, mode system enhancements, and compiler bug fixes

entry.title: Week 42, 2025: Metaprogramming quotation fixes, type system improvements, and compiler debugger enhancements merged this week
entry.content:
Summary

Metaprogramming work and Flambda2 optimizations dominate activity with 10 new PRs opened and ongoing work on modes and effects

Activity

  • New PRs opened this week include continuation handling improvements by Aspen Smith (#4901), metaprogramming mdx fixes by Mark Shinwell (#4900, #4899), Unix local/read annotations by Vesa Karvonen (#4830), preemption support (#4881), CFG value propagation for floats by Xavier Clerc (#4879), extended CFG terminator simplification (#4807), and modes work including global/aliased implications by @dkalinichenko-js (#4889), mode variable conversion (#4792), exception crossing improvements (#4891), and external array renaming (#4890)
  • Metaprogramming infrastructure advanced with quotation evaluation fixes merged by Jack Rickard addressing exception handling and primitive addresses (#4896), double-linking prevention for eval support files by Mark Shinwell (#4895), and ongoing work on Camlinternaleval dependencies (#4899, #4900) after closing superseded work (#4894)
  • Flambda2 optimization work delivered n-way join analysis exposure by Basile Clément (#4821), datalog Virtual_machine.iterator cleanup (#4897), and reverted static constant sharing due to backtrace issues (#4893) though it was briefly merged (#4800)
  • Backend and type system improvements included CFG terminator simplification revert due to loop information issues by Xavier Clerc (#4885), private Reg.t refactoring in progress by Greta Yorsh (#4888), bits8/bits16 kinds compatibility by Nailen Matschke (#4898), and path loading fix by Andrei Odintsov (#4878)
  • Infrastructure and tooling updates included GDB memsearch utility improvements by Nick Barnes (#4886), const qualifiers for camlstartup functions (#4882), cfgtool.exe development for debugging CFG issues (#4596), config variables printing work (#4873), toplevel expunging fix by David Allsopp (#4877), and shrink-wrapping CI testing (#4892)
  • Ongoing runtime and effects work includes domain lock unification by Stephen Dolan (#4769), preemption implementation by Aspen Smith (#4881), continuation handling improvements (#4901), and GC check skipping for external arrays (#4622)
entry.summary: Metaprogramming quotation fixes, type system improvements, and compiler debugger enhancements merged this week

entry.title: Week 41, 2025: Runtime quotations and js_of_ocaml integration landed alongside DWARF debugging improvements and block indices moving to stable
entry.content:
Summary

23 new PRs opened including preemption, staticity mode axis, and n-way join analysis, plus metaprogramming and JavaScript backend work completed

Activity

  • Twenty-three new PRs opened this week representing future work: preemption by Aspen Smith (#4881), staticity mode axis by Zesen Qian (#4799), n-way join analysis exposure by Basile Clément (#4821), extended CFG terminator simplification by Xavier Clerc (#4807), Unix params annotations by Vesa Karvonen (#4830), -quiet flag for ocamldebug by Jakub Bachurski (#4869), print all config variables by Greta Yorsh (#4873), workflow promote failed diffs by Xavier Clerc (#4808), reversible name mangling by Tim McGilchrist (#4850), Classic mode fallback inlining switch-off by Vincent Laviron (#4845), CI warning addition by Jacob Van Buren (#4867), ocamlj testing and CI by Jacob Van Buren (#4753), compiler and driver changes for jsofocaml by Jacob Van Buren (#4791), automatic module field reordering for mixed modules by James Rayman (#4020), flambda2 test tools building by Basile Clément (#4851), stdlib.mli stateless annotations by @dkalinichenko-js (#4839), slambda with trivial transl and eval by Luke Maurer (#4776), levels for variable sorting by Andrej Ivašković (#4785), JSIR naked int32 array initialization optimization by Leo Lee (#4741), parsing crossings support by Zack Eisbach (#4781), mode to constant conversion for cmi by Zesen Qian (#4792), prototype ikind system by @julesjacobs (#4675), and metrics upload artifacts exploration by Jacob Van Buren (#4848)
  • Metaprogramming infrastructure delivered through runtime quotations with new <[ ]> syntax and $ splicing by Andrej Ivašković merging foundational PR with 9,312 additions (#4395), parse tree quotation nodes by Andrej Ivašković (#4852), backend driver support by Jack Rickard and Mark Shinwell (#4864), quotation evaluation by Jack Rickard (#4874), and ocaml-jit integration by Jack Rickard (#4855, #4856)
  • JavaScript backend maturation through complete jsofocaml compiler import with 303,437 additions by Jacob Van Buren (#4730), JSIR symbol imports compilation unit by Leo Lee (#4737), bytecode primitives in JSIR mode by Leo Lee (#4739), and JSIR documentation by Leo Lee (#4743)
  • DWARF debugging improvements encompassing register availability analysis fixes by Mark Shinwell and Greta Yorsh (#4835, #4870), experimental codegen flag by Mark Shinwell (#4871), Alias binding substitution restriction by Mark Shinwell (#4872), inlined frames for specialised code by Vincent Laviron fixing #4831 (#4842), spill placement fixes by Mark Shinwell (#4837), Namefordebugger at tailrec_entry by Greta Yorsh (#4863), dummy probe notes by Greta Yorsh (#4834), and test identifier filtering by Simon Spies (#4829)
  • Type system and unboxed types work including block indices moving to stable by Ryan Tjoa (#4827), atomic fields disallowance by Ryan Tjoa (#4824), flattened float fields with unboxed types by Ryan Tjoa (#4826), unsafe pointer primitives by Ryan Tjoa (#4828), Lambda layouts externality use by Jakub Bachurski and Ryan Tjoa (#4853), polymorphic variants with-bounds restoration by Liam Stevenson and Richard Eisenberg (#4847), jkind normalization fix by Liam Stevenson (#4777), mode system refactoring by Zesen Qian (#4790), atomic fields in mixed blocks forbidding by Aspen Smith (#4868), and Multicore.spawn soundness fix by Vesa Karvonen (#4875)
  • Backend and compilation improvements with CFG terminator simplification by Xavier Clerc (#4772), optcomp backend parameterization by Jacob Van Buren (#4825), asmlink file splitting by Jacob Van Buren (#4838), location-based Reg.t containers by Basile Clément (#4865), Flambda nullable kinds printer by Basile Clément (#4823), and Bytes.get/String.get bytecode differentiation by Jérôme Vouillon (#4782)
  • CI and build system enhancements through dependency simplification by Jacob Van Buren (#4833), Dune max version check removal by Xavier Clerc (#4866), ocamlformat speedups by Jacob Van Buren (#4857), further CI acceleration by Jacob Van Buren (#4858), macOS CI load reduction by Jacob Van Buren (#4859), workflow conditional syntax fixing by Jacob Van Buren (#4860), diff application workflow by Xavier Clerc (#4810), Nix flake github-actions by George Shammas (#4711), Dune version update in default.nix by Jacob Van Buren (#4846), and metrics script fix by @spiessimon merged as #4844
  • Tooling and quality improvements with AGENTS.md documentation by Jacob Van Buren (#4849), type path printing penalization by Nailen Matschke (#4832), zero_alloc warning scope fix by Greta Yorsh (#4819), version bump to 5.2.0minus-22 by Mark Shinwell (#4876), over-expunged toplevel fix by David Allsopp (#4877), and code formatting by Jacob Van Buren (#4843, #4836)
entry.summary: Runtime quotations and js_of_ocaml integration landed alongside DWARF debugging improvements and block indices moving to stable

entry.title: Week 40, 2025: Datalog infrastructure improvements, js_of_ocaml integration, mode system evolution, and match-in-match bug fixes
entry.content:
Summary

Focus on jsofocaml integration, mode system refactoring with new staticity axis, datalog improvements, and block indices stabilization

Activity

  • Jsofocaml integration progressing with compiler and driver changes by Jacob Van Buren generating extensive discussion with 46 comments (#4791), plus sourcemap fixes (#4792) and linking optimizations (#4793)
  • Mode system refactoring by Zesen Qian to simplify adding new axes (#4789), convert modes to constants in .cmi files (#4790), and introduce new staticity mode axis with active discussion (#4799)
